channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: Lebanese emergency services are overwhelmed and need better gear to save lives in wartime
item.description: The civil defense forces of one of the world’s most war-torn nations are shocked at the destruction underway in Lebanon.
item.dc:creator: Sarah El Deeb, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Mexico's Sheinbaum will keep doing mañanera briefings, though her style is unlike her predecessor's
item.description: MEXICO CITY (AP) — Mexico's new President, Claudia Sheinbaum, will start her day Wednesday much like her political mentor, ex-President Andrés Manuel López Obrador, began most of his: with an early morning press briefing from the National Palace.
item.dc:creator: FABIOLA SÁNCHEZ, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Thai police arrest driver and work to identify victims of the school bus fire that killed 23
item.description: BANGKOK (AP) — Thai police have arrested the driver of a bus carrying young students and teachers that caught fire and killed 23 in suburban Bangkok, as families arrived in the capital Wednesday to help identify their loved ones.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Palestinians in West Bank risk crossing Israel's separation barrier to flee failing economy
item.description: YATTA, West Bank (AP) — At dawn in mid-May, Sayyed Ayyed and dozens of other unemployed Palestinian men gathered at the foot of the towering wall of concrete and barbed wire dividing the occupied West Bank from Israel. A smuggler was there with a ladder and ropes.
item.dc:creator: JACK JEFFERY, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: What to know about Iran's missile barrage and Israel's ground operations in Lebanon
item.description: JERUSALEM (AP) — Iran launched at least 180 missiles at Israel on Tuesday evening, causing scattered damage and fires from falling shrapnel, but Israeli authorities said there were no injuries. An Israeli security official said most of the missiles were intercepted, though some managed to land.
item.dc:creator: JULIA FRANKEL, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: White House calls Iranian missile attack 'significant escalation'
item.description: White House national security adviser Jake Sullivan on Tuesday called Iran's missile attack against Israel a "significant escalation" and said the Biden administration is determining how to respond.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, The Hill

item.title: 6 people killed in Tel Aviv shooting attack
item.description: Six civilians were killed Tuesday in a shooting in Tel Aviv, local police said, as tensions between Israel and Iran-backed terrorist groups in the region have dramatically escalated in recent days and weeks.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, The Hill

item.title: What to know about Hezbollah's capabilities after its recent losses
item.description: BEIRUT (AP) — Hezbollah has suffered some of the heaviest losses in its history over the past two weeks, chief among them the killing of its longtime leader, Hassan Nasrallah, in an Israeli airstrike.
item.dc:creator: BASSEM MROUE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: How does Israel’s Iron Dome work?
item.description: Iran launched missiles toward Israel Tuesday, just days after Hezbollah leader Hassan Nasrallah was killed in a strike on Lebanon. The Iranian blitz comes after a nearly yearlong cross-border conflict between Hezbollah, a star proxy group for Tehran, and Israeli forces.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, The Hill

item.title: Mexico's Claudia Sheinbaum sworn in as North America's first female head of state
item.description: Mexican President Claudia Sheinbaum was sworn in at a joint session of the country’s Congress on Tuesday, becoming North America’s first female head of state.
item.dc:creator: Rafael Bernal, The Hill

item.title: Yemen's Houthi rebels launch drone boat that hits ship in Red Sea as missile strikes another
item.description: DUBAI, United Arab Emirates (AP) — Yemen's Houthi rebels launched an explosive-loaded drone that crashed into one ship Tuesday in the Red Sea and a missile that exploded against another.
item.dc:creator: JON GAMBRELL, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Russian fighter jet flies within feet of US F-16 near Alaska
item.description: The U.S. military is slamming Russia for an “unsafe” encounter in the skies off the coast of Alaska after footage released Monday shows a Kremlin fighter jet cutting off an Air Force F-16. The incident, which took place Sept.
item.dc:creator: Ellen Mitchell, The Hill

item.title: US Embassy in Israel warns employees to shelter in place amid threat of Iran missile attack
item.description: The U.S. Embassy in Jerusalem is cautioning all government employees and their family members to shelter in place until further notice as Iran prepares to launch missiles at Israel. The warning came as U.S. officials said they expected Iran to attack Israel with missile and drone strikes on Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, The Hill

item.title: Archaeologists discover 5,000-year-old ancient community in Morocco
item.description: An ancient farming community was discovered in Morocco. Items found around the 5,000-year-old community included animal remains, pottery, stone, axes and more.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Iran preparing imminent missile attack on Israel, US warns, pledging 'severe consequences'
item.description: JERUSALEM (AP) — Iran is preparing to “imminently” launch a ballistic missile attack on Israel, according to a senior U.S. administration official, who warned Tuesday of “severe consequences” should it take place.
item.dc:creator: AAMER MADHANI, JULIA FRANKEL and BASSEM MROUE, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: More than 20 are feared dead after a school bus catches fire in suburban Bangkok
item.description: BANGKOK (AP) — A bus carrying young students and their teachers on a school trip caught fire in suburban Bangkok on Tuesday, leaving more than 20 feared dead, officials and rescuers said.
item.dc:creator: JINTAMAS SAKSORNCHAI, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: NATO's new chief makes Ukraine support a top priority and says he will work with any US president
item.description: BRUSSELS (AP) — The new head of NATO vowed on Tuesday to help shore up Western support for war-ravaged Ukraine and expressed confidence that he can work with whoever is elected president of the United States, the alliance's most powerful member, in November.
item.dc:creator: LORNE COOK, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: WikiLeaks' Julian Assange says he pleaded 'guilty to journalism' in order to be freed
item.description: STRASBOURG, France (AP) — WikiLeaks founder Julian Assange said Tuesday that he was freed after years of incarceration because he "pled guilty to journalism.
item.dc:creator: PASCAL BASTIEN, BARBARA SURK and SYLVIA HUI, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: A likely Russian artillery strike kills at least 6 at a Ukrainian market
item.description: KYIV, Ukraine (AP) — An apparent Russian artillery strike hit a market in the southern Ukraine city of Kherson on Tuesday, killing at least six people and wounding three others, authorities said, on a morning when Ukrainians across the country were observing a minute’s silence for their military and
item.dc:creator: HANNA ARHIROVA and ILLIA NOVIKOV, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: South Korea unveils its most powerful missile, which could reach North Korea's underground bunkers
item.description: SEOUL, South Korea (AP) — South Korea unveiled its most powerful ballistic missile and other weapons that could target North Korea during a massive Armed Forces Day ceremony on Tuesday, as the South's president warned the North's regime would collapse if it attempts to use nuclear weapons.
item.dc:creator: HYUNG-JIN KIM, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: A cruise ship bound on a round-the-world voyage leaves Belfast after a 4-month delay
item.description: LONDON (AP) — A cruise ship taking passengers on a 3 ½-year around-the-world voyage has finally sailed from Belfast's dockside, after spending four months stuck in the Northern Ireland capital.
item.dc:creator: JILL LAWLESS, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Taiwan shuts schools and offices ahead a direct hit from powerful typhoon
item.description: KAOHSIUNG, Taiwan (AP) — Taiwan closed schools and offices and evacuated hundreds from vulnerable areas around the island Tuesday ahead of a strong typhoon expected to hit its densely populated western coast after lashing northern Philippine islands.
item.dc:creator: SIMINA MISTREANU, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Video shows Russian fighter jet flying within feet of US F-16 near Alaska
item.description: The release of the video comes after a series of Russian incursions into the Alaska Air Defense Identification Zone.
item.dc:creator: MARK THIESSEN, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: China marks 75 years of Communist Party rule as economic challenges and security threats linger
item.description: China is marking the 75th year of Communist Party rule as economic challenges and security threats linger over the massive state.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Death toll rises to 18 in South Africa mass shootings, police say
item.description: The death toll from mass shootings at two houses on the same street in a South African village over the weekend has risen to 18, according to officials.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Israel's ground invasion into Lebanon imminent as cabinet approves next phase of the war
item.description: President Biden demanded a 'ceasefire now' following reports that Washington had been briefed by Israel on its plans to launch a 'limited' ground invasion into Lebanon imminently in fight with Hezbollah.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Hezbollah's acting leader vows to fight on after Nasrallah's death
item.description: BEIRUT (AP) — Hezbollah's acting leader vowed Monday to continue battling Israel and said the militant group was prepared for a long fight even after much of its top command was wiped out, including its leader, Hassan Nasrallah.
item.dc:creator: ABBY SEWELL and NATALIE MELZER, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Who were the 7 high-ranking Hezbollah officials killed over the past week?
item.description: The move left Lebanon and much of the Mideast in shock as Israeli officials celebrated major military breakthroughs.
item.dc:creator: KAREEM CHEHAYEB and JACK JEFFERY, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: US airstrikes on Syria kill 37 militants affiliated with extremist groups
item.description: BEIRUT (AP) — Two U.S. airstrikes in Syria killed 37 militants affiliated with the Islamic State group and an al-Qaeda-linked group, the U.S. military said Sunday. It said two of the dead were senior militants. U.S.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Biden says he will talk to Netanyahu as Israel pummels Sunni terror targets in Beirut
item.description: The Israel Defense Forces reportedly began striking the Lebanese capital of Beirut on Sunday evening after President Biden said he intended to speak with Prime Minister Netanyahu.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Iran's Ayatollah Ali Khamenei in hiding with extra security following Hezbollah leader's death: report
item.description: Iran's Supreme Leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei was placed in a secure location after Hezbollah leader Hassan Nasrallah's killing in Lebanon.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Hezbollah confirms its leader Hassan Nasrallah was killed in an Israeli airstrike
item.description: BEIRUT (AP) — Lebanon’s Hezbollah group confirmed on Saturday that its leader and one of its founding members, Hassan Nasrallah, was killed in an Israeli airstrike in a southern suburb of Beirut.
item.dc:creator: BASSEM MROUE and MELANIE LIDMAN, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Iranian operatives charged with hacking Trump's presidential campaign
item.description: The operatives are accused of hacking Trump's campaign and disseminating stolen information to media organizations.
item.dc:creator: ERIC TUCKER, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Netanyahu vows to use 'full force' against Hezbollah and dims hopes for a cease-fire
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu on Thursday vowed to carry out “full force” strikes against Hezbollah until it ceases firing rockets across the border, dimming hopes for a cease-fire proposal put forth by U.S. and European officials.
item.dc:creator: TIA GOLDENBERG, BASSEM MROUE and MELANIE LIDMAN, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Harris blasts proposals for Ukraine to cede territory to Russia during Zelenskyy meeting
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — With American support for Ukraine at a partisan crossroads, Vice President Kamala Harris on Thursday slammed suggestions that Kyiv should cede territory for the sake of peace with Moscow as “dangerous and unacceptable.
item.dc:creator: CHRIS MEGERIAN, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: US and allies call for an immediate 21-day cease-fire between Israel and Hezbollah
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — The U.S., France and other allies jointly called Wednesday for an immediate 21-day cease-fire to allow for negotiations in the escalating conflict between Israel and Hezbollah that has killed more than 600 people in Lebanon in recent days.
item.dc:creator: AAMER MADHANI, MATTHEW LEE and JENNIFER PELTZ, Associated Press, NewsNation
